title: Rei I Unit 00 Theory Discussion
wikitext:
[]: Rei I = Unit-00 Theory Discussion             Quote Post by The Abhorrent » Wed Feb 09, 2011 10:08 am Split from Rei's Betrayal . NemZ wrote: Wait... so you're saying she was only fragmented a few days before the series starts? I'm not sure I buy that. This soul-splitting would seem like the kind of thing that would really mess with your personality (Kyoko much?), and thus is a good explanation in and of itself why she's so disconnected from everything. The problem is that we know she's been in school for a year prior to the start and has always been like that, so are we to assume that becoming half-souled doesn't mess with her enough for her classmates to notice anything weird(er) going on with her?

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Wed Feb 09, 2011 10:50 am The Abhorrent wrote: Still, it's just a theory on my part. It is generally agreed upon that a fragment of Rei is within Eva-00, though it's very hard to pin down when or how it was placed within the Eva. In my opinion, the contact experiment just prior to the series is the best explanation. Still, there's still a significant time gap which may allow for another method (which is never mentioned in the series. Another (earlier) experiment is also possible, but I'm not sure if the time when Eva-00 went berserk is stated to be her first time within the unit. If it is not, perhaps what happened was that the part of Rei which had been stuck inside the Eva managed to gain some control (temporarily) and show Gendo how angry she was with him.

[]:             Quote Post by The Abhorrent » Wed Feb 09, 2011 11:16 am thewayneiac wrote: If a porton of Rei 2's soul had been recently removed to ensoul Unit-00, Unit-00's soul would look like Rei 2. That it is eventually identified as Rei 1 indicates that the soul did not age inside Rei 2 for any significant amount of time.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Feb 09, 2011 3:25 pm I somewhat suspect the split was made while Rei1 was dead, actually. It's gotta be easier to work with a soul when it isn't busy, right?

[]:             Quote Post by The Abhorrent » Wed Feb 09, 2011 4:01 pm NemZ wrote: I somewhat suspect the split was made while Rei1 was dead, actually. It's gotta be easier to work with a soul when it isn't busy, right?

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Wed Feb 09, 2011 4:45 pm The Abhorrent wrote: This is largely a timeline issue, as it's not indicated when the activation experimentation occurred (to my knowledge at least, didn't find any info for it on the wiki either)

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Wed Feb 09, 2011 5:58 pm Azathoth wrote: It's immediately proceeded by a "TWO WEEKS AGO" timestamp. At least I think it's two weeks. Regardless, seven months it isn't.

[]:             Quote Post by Imari » Mon Feb 14, 2011 8:24 am The easiest explanation for me is Rei I gets strangled, soul ends up in Rei II and shortly afterwards is involved in a contact experiment. This gets around the needing a live donor problem, which all evidence so far suggests is true. There's no suggestion that NERV or anyone can handle souls outside of vessels. Because of the timing, the Unit-00 part of the soul still identifies itself as Rei I, and Rei II develops along new lines herself with what is left of the soul. Is there any evidence that Rei II remembers anything of her life as Rei I? Rei III obviously remembers being Rei II. If she doesn't, then it could be explained by the trauma of the contact experiment.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Feb 14, 2011 10:17 am Imari wrote: There's no suggestion that NERV or anyone can handle souls outside of vessels.

[]:             Quote Post by Imari » Tue Feb 15, 2011 1:57 am Reichu wrote: A whole classroom of "4th Level Candidates" are implied to be able to have cores prepared for them upon being selected by the Marduk Institution. Unless you believe Nerv has their moms all alive and locked up somewhere, it's simpler to think that they have corpses on ice or souls in "jars" or something.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Feb 15, 2011 2:36 am Before Japan receives Eva-03, Ritsuko says that there's a boy for whom a core can be prepared immediately, i.e., they don't have a core ready yet.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue Feb 15, 2011 6:48 am Nerv is likely to rely upon Seele for their "soul jars", since only Toji's mom's soul was available with them at the time Eva-03 was ready, and since Seele could ready cores for nine MP Evas when necessary. Seele stores the souls. Rei's soul didn't need to be sent into Eva-00 so quickly after the whole strangulation incident: while one part probably went into Rei 2's body, the Rei 1 part was probably put in a "soul jar" for quite a while. To put Rei 1's crazy segment into Eva-00's core too early would be asking for a disaster.

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Tue Feb 15, 2011 7:32 am UrsusArctos wrote: Nerv is likely to rely upon Seele for their "soul jars", since only Toji's mom's soul was available with them at the time Eva-03 was ready, and since Seele could ready cores for nine MP Evas when necessary. Seele stores the souls.

[]: If Unit-00 has Rei I's soul...             Quote Post by Replicator » Tue Oct 16, 2012 8:36 pm On the EvaWiki, the generally accepted theory regarding Unit-00's soul, is that it contains Rei I's, which was apparently implanted after her death. But isn't there a continuity error here? Rei was supposedly created for the purpose of housing Lilith's soul , so how would Rei I's soul (who's body is also presumably grown from Lilith) be planted in Unit-00, and Rei II still comes along in NGE? Basically, what I am saying is, Lilith's soul, by way of Rei I would have to inhabit Unit-00. Of course, soul divisibility may come into play here, and Gendo could have merely divided Lilith/Rei's soul for use in the next incarnation of Rei. Also, I vaguely remember Rei III as being described as having "the complete soul of Lilith", so it could've been possible Unit-00's resident soul was salvaged and placed into Rei III. Discuss...

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Oct 16, 2012 9:37 pm Souls can be divided in the Evaverse. Rei can sense her own presence within Unit 00, and Shinji can feel Rei within it as well. Rei III seems to have the ability to talk to her past lives in her head when she's by herself, indicating a complete soul.

[]: Re: If Unit-00 has Rei I's soul...             Quote Post by Lurkis » Tue Oct 16, 2012 10:47 pm Replicator wrote: so how would Rei I's soul (who's body is also presumably grown from Lilith)

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Oct 17, 2012 4:23 am Doesn't mean she wasn't grown out of Lilith. Eva 01 grew out of her despite being an Adam clone.

[]:             Quote Post by Lurkis » Wed Oct 17, 2012 10:36 am AuraTwilight wrote: Doesn't mean she wasn't grown out of Lilith. Eva 01 grew out of her despite being an Adam clone.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Oct 17, 2012 10:51 am I still find the notion that Rei I wound up in Unit 00 sketchy. In fact, I find all of the soul recycling a bit problematic; it's clear all the Reis and Unit 00 have shards of Lilith's soul, but I've yet to see a compelling argument that one particular shard was ferried about from one vessel to another. IMO it's more likely that each shard was independent of the rest, and that the lot were collected into Lilith prior to 3I. This would explain why we see three Reis during both endings, along with the chibi version seen in Unit 00 -- after all, if it was Rei I it would be a bit odd for it to show up onscreen at the same time as the latter, no? We know there have to be at least three fragments out there (Rei II, Unit 00, and Lilith), so why not go for broke and just say each incarnation of Rei has a different soul fragment? Seems to me it'd make things a lot easier overall.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Oct 17, 2012 1:12 pm Eva 01 isn't an Adam clone, the rest of them are whereas 01 is the only Eva to be cloned directly from Lilith.

Another (earlier) experiment is also possible, but I'm not sure if the time when Eva-00 went berserk is stated to be her first time within the unit. If it is not, perhaps what happened was that the part of Rei which had been stuck inside the Eva managed to gain some control (temporarily) and show Gendo how angry she was with him.
